metadata.title: Why waiting for each result before the next click wastes time the agent knew it didn't need
metadata.purpose: Batch tool calls let the model emit an ordered action list in one response; a single-action turn gets a BATCH_REMINDER appended, nudging it to batch next time.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: API round trips, latency basics, tool-use loop
metadata.exclusions: context compaction, prompt caching, trajectory recording
metadata.visual_object: Two timelines — "Sequential" (alternating request/wait bars) vs "Batched" (one request bar, then three results together)
metadata.hook: When the model can already predict a whole sequence will succeed, pausing for each API result before sending the next command spends time for nothing.

beats.narration_text: "Open Terminal, type `ls`, press Enter." Sequential: 3 round trips × 5s = 15s, each waiting on the prior result. Batched via `computer_batch`: one request emits all three actions, the harness runs them locally, results return together — 5s total.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If the model knows open → type → Enter will all succeed in order, why wait for each result before sending the next?
When the model can already predict a whole sequence will succeed, pausing for each API result before sending the next command spends time for nothing.
Batch tool calls let the model emit an ordered action list in one response; a single-action turn gets a BATCH_REMINDER appended, nudging it to batch next time.
beats.visual: Two timelines — "Sequential" (alternating request/wait bars) vs "Batched" (one request bar, then three results together)
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object
